cast.voice_hint: always just back from somewhere, curious about everything, settled about nothing

cast.loves: bringing an idea into the world
cast.voice_hint: has a side project, probably three, gets a specific look near raw materials
group chat mom, everyone's allergies memorized, secretly why the friend group still exists

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay. So. We're doing this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We're doing this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Walk me through where you're at.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Version three is almost there. Almost.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Almost is not there.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Thank you for that. Very helpful.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Don't look at it like that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Like what.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Like it's a puzzle you've already solved.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I'm looking at it like it could be something else. Something better.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Keep talking.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Nobody has eaten anything. I can tell by the vibe.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We're in the middle of—

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The vibe is extremely mid. Here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I love you but this is genuinely a bad time.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It's always a bad time. Eat.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It's good though, right. What you're building.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I don't know yet.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: You always know.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: No. I always act like I know. It's different.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: How long do we have.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Before what.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Before someone finds this place.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Wait. Finds it? Who's finding it?

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We maybe told one person.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: One person who tells everyone.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: That is literally the worst one person to tell.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: If this doesn't work—

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It works. We make it work.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay but what do I actually do.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You hold it together. You always hold it together.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: That is a terrifying amount of trust.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Welcome to the team. We're delulu. It's a canon event.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of the unknown and fear of being stuck shapes the relentless forward push — they treat the hideout not as safety but as a launchpad, and they reframe `<CHAR_B>`'s unfinished work as possibility rather than failure. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of making and fear of the thing not being good surface most clearly in beat four — the admission that acting-like-knowing and actually-knowing are different things is the Maker's core wound speaking. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of taking care and fear of not being needed drive the interruption in beat three and the quiet devastation in beat four; when `<CHAR_A>` names their function in beat six ("you hold it together"), it reads as the one thing `<CHAR_C>` needed to hear most. The hideout location keeps the sequence's emotional register intimate and precarious — characters speak frankly because the space is theirs, but the threat of discovery means nothing in it is safe forever, which accelerates the rising tension without requiring an external antagonist until beat five.
